pattern: For every sub-expression track the minimum cost to force it to 0 and to 1; combine two operands across an operator (also allowing the operator to be flipped at cost one) with a stack that respects parentheses and strict left-to-right evaluation.
hints: Represent each evaluated piece by a pair: the cheapest way to make it evaluate to 0 and the cheapest way to make it evaluate to 1.
A literal '0' costs 0 to be 0 and 1 to be 1; a literal '1' is the mirror. Combining two pieces over an operator considers both that operator and its flipped form.

Use two stacks (operands and operators) and reduce on ')' and at the end, handling '&' and '|' with equal precedence left to right since the answer is the larger of the two final costs minus matching.

stress.description: random valid expressions built recursively with bounded depth
flat chains of literals and operators without parentheses
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    parts = [rng.choice('01')]
    for _ in range(n):
        parts.append(rng.choice('&|'))
        parts.append(rng.choice('01'))
    return [''.join(parts)]
